program_name: Comparative International Relations - European Union Studies and Global Studies
teaching_language: English for the European Union Studies and Global Studies curricula [uncertain]

academic_requirements.summary: Open-admission master's degree for the English-taught European Union Studies and Global Studies curricula. Applicants need a three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent, must satisfy specific curricular requirements, must demonstrate adequate personal preparation including a second language at B1 level, and must meet the English-language requirement. International applicants must also undergo the preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree, or an equivalent foreign qualification of at least three years that is valid for master's-level access.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must have at least 36 ECTS or equivalent distributed as follows: 6 ECTS in law; 6 ECTS in economics; 6 ECTS in contemporary history, history of Islamic countries, history of Eastern and South-Eastern Asia, or economic history; 6 ECTS in history of economic thought, history and institutions of America, history of international relations, history and institutions of Africa, or history and institutions of Asia; plus 12 additional ECTS in one or more of those same fields.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: The apply.unive.it course page states a minimum CGPA threshold of 70% for international evaluation. The programme admission page also says that candidates who already hold a recognised second-language certification and have a bachelor's final grade of 90/110 are generally considered to possess adequate personal preparation.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: The Teaching Committee evaluates the undergraduate transcript, curriculum vitae and studiorum, and second-language evidence. If preparation cannot be assessed unequivocally, the Committee may require an integrative interview. For international qualifications, the Welcome Unit first carries out the preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it.

language_requirements.required_level: English at minimum B2 level, plus a second language at minimum B1 level for the chosen curriculum.
accepted_proofs: For English: a recognised certificate accepted by Ca' Foscari, such as Cambridge FCE/CAE/CPE, Trinity ISE II-IV, IELTS, TOEFL iBT, PTE Academic, Oxford Test of English, LanguageCert ESOL, Aptis ESOL, or a recognised Ca' Foscari CLA certificate.

For the second language: an official language certification, or documentation proving a third-year university language exam, a university exam whose syllabus states a B1 exit level, five years of second-language study in secondary school, or a language course certifying B1 exit level.
exemption_rules: English may be waived through the official Ca' Foscari exemption categories, including prior higher education in an English-medium country, a qualification from a study path taught entirely in English, a full exchange year in an English-medium secondary school, or one of the listed language-degree qualifications.
For European Union Studies, accepted B1 second languages are French, Portuguese, Spanish, or German.
For Global Studies, accepted B1 second languages are French, Portuguese, Russian, or Spanish.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proof may be provided later than the initial application but must be satisfied by the enrolment deadline. The programme page treats second-language proof as part of the personal-preparation assessment, while the official apply.unive.it tips article says Comparative International Relations applicants must include B1 second-language certification at submission [uncertain].
language_requirements.deadline: English by the enrolment deadline; second-language proof at application/evaluation stage [uncertain].

rounds.deadline: Successful applicants may enrol from July 1, 2026.
rounds.notes: [uncertain] These are the programme's official 2026 evaluation dates. International-qualification applicants who do not require a visa must also respect the citizenship-based apply.unive.it rounds.

rounds.deadline: Admitted candidates may use the ordinary or additional enrolment window.
rounds.notes: [uncertain] The static public apply.unive.it EU-round text does not expose the full 2026/2027 round-by-round timetable.

rounds.deadline: This session runs within the programme's additional enrolment period.
rounds.notes: [uncertain] Late-session candidates rely on the additional enrolment window.

application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Ordinary enrolment window: July 1, 2026 to September 30, 2026. Additional enrolment window with a 50 EUR late fee: October 1, 2026 to December 18, 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: These dates are taken from the official programme admission page. International applicants with foreign qualifications must also complete the preliminary evaluation through apply.unive.it.

rounds.application_window: [uncertain] Ca' Foscari says 2026/2027 applications began on December 17, 2025, but the static public article does not display the detailed visa-round windows.
rounds.results_publication: [uncertain] Master's applicants generally receive feedback about 8 weeks after the round in which they applied.
rounds.deadline: As of June 5, 2026, the official apply.unive.it article states that visa-requiring non-EU applicants can no longer apply for 2026/2027.
rounds.notes: The public article gives the current status but not the underlying historical round calendar.

application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: Universitaly pre-enrolment for 2026/2027 must be submitted by September 30, 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: Closed as of June 5, 2026 for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Applicants already residing in Italy with a permit valid for university enrolment must instead follow the EU/equivalent pathway.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or, for European applicants, valid ID card
required_documents.notes: A renewal or first-request receipt is also accepted where officially stated.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or renewal/first-request receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-European applicants already residing in Italy or in the Schengen Area.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records for all undergraduate years
required_documents.notes: A provisional transcript is acceptable if the applicant is still completing the degree.

required_documents.document_name: Official English or Italian translations of transcript and diploma documents
required_documents.notes: Required when the originals are not in English or Italian.
Mandatory for master's-level international applications and used in the programme's personal-preparation assessment.

required_documents.document_name: Motivation letter dedicated to Comparative International Relations
required_documents.notes: The apply.unive.it course page explicitly requests a dedicated motivation letter for this programme.

required_documents.document_name: B1 second-language certification or accepted alternative proof
required_documents.notes: The programme page accepts certification or specific documentary alternatives; the official apply.unive.it tips article says Comparative International Relations applicants must submit B1 second-language certification with the application [uncertain].

required_documents.document_name: Recognised B2 English certificate or evidence of exemption
required_documents.notes: English must be satisfied by the enrolment deadline even if the applicant applies before obtaining the certificate.
If unavailable during application, the final diploma is needed later to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: CIMEA Statement of Comparability, or Declaration of Value, or ARDI Statement of Correspondence with CIMEA Statement of Verification, or Diploma Supplement
required_documents.notes: Ca' Foscari strongly recommends obtaining one of these qualification-recognition documents before application, and one accepted route is required to complete enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard admission test; the Teaching Committee may require an integrative interview
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants are assessed through curricular and personal-preparation evaluation. An additional interview is only used when the Teaching Committee cannot assess preparation unequivocally.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No fixed 2026 interview calendar is published on the programme page. Evaluation sessions run April-June, July-August, and October-November 2026.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: No ranking-test score is published. The programme page says candidates with second-language certification and a bachelor's final grade of 90/110 are generally considered to possess adequate personal preparation; the apply.unive.it course page separately states a minimum CGPA of 70% for international evaluation.

source_quotes.quote: Comparative International Relations - European Union Studies and Global Studies
Candidates must have achieved minimum 36 CFU
A minimum B1-level knowledge of a second language is also required

uncertainty_notes: The degree-programme home page describes Comparative International Relations generally as Italian/English, while the international-programmes list and the apply.unive.it course page clearly identify the European Union Studies and Global Studies curricula as the English-taught options targeted here.

The timing of second-language proof is not perfectly aligned across official sources: the programme page treats it as part of the personal-preparation assessment, while the apply.unive.it tips article says Comparative International Relations applicants must include B1 second-language certification at submission.
The public apply.unive.it visa-round article confirms closure status for 2026/2027 but does not expose the detailed visa-round windows in accessible static text.
